name: From Castelló to Benicàssim – Circular via the Paseo Marítimo
name_original: Desde Castelló hasta Benicàssim – Circular por el Paseo Marítimo
region: Castellón De La Plana / Castelló De La Plana
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Castellón De La Plana / Castelló De La Plana traverse a diverse landscape, from the Mediterranean coast to rugged inland mountains. The region features extensive natural parks, including Sierra de Irta and Desert de les Palmes, offering varied terrain for cyclists. Old railway lines have been converted into greenways, providing accessible and scenic paths. This blend of coastal routes, challenging mountain climbs, and protected natural areas makes Castellón De La Plana / Castelló De La Plana suitable for a wide range of touring cycling experiences. Best touring cycling routes around Castellón De La Plana / Castelló De La Plana The most popular touring cycling route is From Castelló to Benicàssim – Circular via the Paseo Marítimo, an easy 18.0 miles (29.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 56 minutes to complete. This route offers flat coastal riding with sea views between Castelló and Benicàssim. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Via Verde Oropesa Benicassim – Tunnel de Capicorb loop from Castelló, a moderate 31.0 miles (49.9 km) path. This route utilizes a scenic greenway along the coast, passing through tunnels and offering refreshing sea breezes. Local touring cyclists also love the Coll de la Mola – Bartolo Summit loop from Castelló, a 24.6 miles (39.6 km) trail leading through the challenging terrain of Desert de les Palmes Natural Park, often completed in about 2 hours 59 minutes. Touring cycling around Castellón De La Plana / Castelló De La Plana is defined by coastal paths, extensive natural parks, and greenways. The network offers options for different ability levels, from leisurely rides to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Castellón De La Plana / Castelló De La Plana are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 390 reviews. More than 4000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Castellón De La Plana / Castelló De La Plana's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Castellón De La Plana?
A: Castellón De La Plana offers a diverse landscape for touring cyclists. You can find everything from entirely flat coastal paths, like the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e934168976/from-castello-to-benicassim-circular-via-the-paseo-maritimo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Castelló to Benicàssim – Circular via the Paseo Marítimo&lt;/a&gt;, to challenging mountain climbs within natural parks such as Desert de les Palmes. The region's unique position between the Mediterranean Sea and rugged inland mountains provides a wide array of experiences.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or casual riders?
A: Yes, there are many easy touring cycling routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed ride. Coastal paths are generally flat and scenic, offering refreshing sea breezes. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31959951" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa del Gurugú – Benicàssim Seafront Cycle Path loop from Castelló&lt;/a&gt;, which is 27.3 km long with minimal elevation gain.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, the inland natural parks offer significant climbs and descents. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14839373" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de la Mola – Bartolo Summit loop from Castelló&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 39.6 km route with substantial elevation gain, leading through the demanding terrain of Desert de les Palmes Natural Park and offering breathtaking panoramic views.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in the region?
A: Absolutely. Many routes, especially those along the coast or on greenways, are suitable for families. These paths are often flat and well-maintained, providing a safe and enjoyable experience. The Via Verde Oropesa Benicassim is a great example, offering accessible and scenic cycling paths converted from old railway lines.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes around Castellón De La Plana?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in the area are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For instance, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e934168976/from-castello-to-benicassim-circular-via-the-paseo-maritimo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Castelló to Benicàssim – Circular via the Paseo Marítimo&lt;/a&gt; offers a convenient circular journey along the coast.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Castellón De La Plana is rich in natural beauty and historical sites. You can cycle through extensive natural parks like Sierra de Irta and Desert de les Palmes. Routes often pass by significant landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/504604" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Saint Michael on Pico Bartolo&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1323360" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bartolo Summit&lt;/a&gt;, or the coastal &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2958303" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa del Gurugú&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features historic castles and watchtowers.

Q: Are there routes with scenic viewpoints?
A: Many routes offer stunning panoramic views, especially those that venture into the natural parks or along the coast. The Desert de les Palmes Natural Park, for example, provides breathtaking vistas from points like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6410383" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Colomer Observatory&lt;/a&gt;. Coastal routes also provide spectacular sea views, such as those found along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/398395" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde del Mar&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Castellón De La Plana?
A: The mild Mediterranean climate of Castellón De La Plana makes it suitable for touring cycling almost all year round. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures, ideal for longer rides. Even in winter, the weather is often mild enough for cycling, while summer can be enjoyed on coastal routes with refreshing sea breezes.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Castellón De La Plana?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 390 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, from serene coastal paths to challenging mountain trails, and the well-maintained greenways. The blend of natural beauty and cultural stops, like historic castles, is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any routes that incorporate greenways?
A: Yes, Castellón De La Plana is home to segments of significant greenways, which are converted old railway lines. These provide accessible and scenic cycling paths. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/22058912" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via Verde Oropesa Benicassim – Tunnel de Capicorb loop from Castelló&lt;/a&gt;, which utilizes a beautiful coastal greenway.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Castellón De La Plana / Castelló De La Plana?
A: There are over 140 touring cycling routes available around Castellón De La Plana / Castelló De La Plana, catering to various skill levels. This includes approximately 50 easy routes, 37 moderate routes, and over 50 difficult routes, ensuring options for every type of cyclist.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through or near historical sites?
A: Many cycling routes in Castellón De La Plana offer opportunities to explore historical sites. For example, routes in Sierra de Irta Natural Park combine sea views with historical elements like castles and watchtowers. The region also features charming towns and the historic town of Peñíscola with its medieval Templar castle, which can be integrated into longer cycling trips.
tips: Wide beach of fine sand ideal to go with children. The advantage is that you can walk along it until you reach Benicassim or El Grao, either by bike or on foot, and from these places it connects with more bike lanes.

Bicycle lane that runs along the entire coast from Castello de la Plana to Benicassim. You can stop at the various beaches that you cross, the walk is very calm and picturesque. Once in Benicassim you can do the tour of the Villas visiting old houses that are very beautiful architecturally.
